content.en:
Comm (Commutator)

Dylan Wang's video http://youtube.com/watch?v=rZiDvDGHfe8

Notation

Pure commutator [A, B] = A B A' B'; conjugate A: B = [A: B] = A B A', where A is called setup move; commutator A: [B, C] = A B C B' C' A'. Move A solves a piece, if A moves it to the correct place it should go (called its home). Commutator can solve every 3-cycle (see 3-Cycle Comm THM). This page only discusses 3-cycle comm. UF sticker is U-sticker on UF piece; UFR sticker is U-sticker on UFR piece.

Pure Comm

Find a piece among 3-cycle pieces that can be solved in one move. If this move affects and only affects 2 pieces, then it is called interchange, and the third piece that interchange does not affect is called odd piece. For convenience, hold interchange layer on bottom. Choose any sticker on odd piece as odd sticker. The current sticker at the home of odd sticker is called target. Moves that moves odd sticker to target is called Insert, if it preserves (i.e., does not affects) all the pieces in the interchange layer except for target piece.

Pure Corner

Scramble = x2 y R U' R' D' R U R' D. Interchange D' solves DBR piece.

Method 1 is [interchange, insert]. Choose LUF sticker as odd sticker, the home of which is DBR, so target is white-red-green sticker. Interchange D' moves target to DFR place, so insert should move LUF sticker to DFR place, which can be R U' R'. Solution = [D', R U' R'].

Method 2 = [insert, interchange']. Insert B U2 B' solves LUF piece. Solution = [B U2 B', D].

Corner comm might have slice moves. For example, [F' R' S U', R2].

Pure Edge

Scramble 1 = x2 y F' U L' U2 F B' R2 F' B L U' F. Interchange M' solves UF piece.

Method 1 is [interchange, insert]. Choose DR sticker as odd sticker, the home of which is FU, so target is yellow-orange sticker. Interchange M' moves target to UB, so insert should move DR to UB, which can be U R2 U'. Solution = [M', U R2 U'].

Method 2 = [insert, interchange']. Insert F R F' solves DR piece. Solution = [F R F', M].

Scramble 2 = x2 y B2 R B R' L U' R' U R L' B. Interchange U solves UF piece.

Method 1 is [interchange, insert]. Choose BL sticker as odd sticker, the home of which is UR, so target is yellow sticker on yellow-red piece. Interchange U moves target to UF, so insert should move BL to UF, which can be F' E F. Solution = [U, F' E F].

Method 2 = [insert, interchange']. Insert R' E2 R solves BL piece. Solution = [R' E2 R, U'].

Edge comm might have slice moves. For example, [U R U' R', D' R' D R].

Conjugate Comm

If there is no interchange possible, or the pure comm is not good, consider conjugate comm.

Scramble 1 = x2 y R' F R' B2 R F' R' B2 R2 x. U' cannot be interchange since it affects all 3 pieces although it solves UFR piece. In fact, there is no interchange possible. However, after setup move R2, interchange can be D2 since it solves LBD piece. Using method 1, insert can be R U R'. Solution = R2: [D2, R U R'].

Scramble 2 = x2 y U2 L U2 D B U B' D' B U' B' U2 L' U2. Solution without setup = [D', R U2 R' U' R U R'] (16), solution 1 with setup = U: [R' B' R, F2] (10), solution 2 with setup = U: [F2, U B2 U'] = U F2 U B2 U' F2 U B2 U2 (10-1).

let G be the free product C_4 * C_4 = <a,b | a^4 = b^4 = 1>. according to GAP, there is a finite quotient of G of order 256 obtained by adding 12 extra relations to the presentation. in this quotient, (abab^-1)^2 is not a commutator (finite computation, just check all possible commutators), so it is also not a commutator in G. therefore (U M U M')2 is not a commutator of U and M moves

(U M U M')2 = (U2: [U', M])2 = [U, M U M' mLR]

U M' U' M is a 5style commutator

algorithm groups are hyperbolic groups.

let G be the free product C_4 * C_4 = <a,b | a^4 = b^4 = 1>. according to GAP, there is a finite quotient of G of order 256 obtained by adding 12 extra relations to the presentation. in this quotient, (abab^-1)^2 is not a commutator (finite computation, just check all possible commutators), so it is also not a commutator in G. therefore (U M U M')2 is not a commutator of U and M moves.

S = <a,b> is the set of all "words" where the allowed letters are a, a^-1, b, b^-1. for example, s = aababbbbbba^-1a^-1b^-1a = a^2 b a b^6 a^-2 b^-1 a is a word in S

G = <a,b | a^4 = b^4 = 1> is the same set but where you have the simplification rules a^4=1 and b^4=1. so in G, the word s can be simplified to a^2 b a b^2 a^2 b^-1, because b^6 = b^2 and a^-2 = a^2

G is the group of 2gen algorithms. we consider words/algorithms that can be simplified to each other as the same algorithm, but different if they can't be simplified to each other (even if they both do the same thing on the cube. we're only considering the algorithms as abstract strings of symbols, not as permutations of pieces/stickers)

G obviously contains infinitely many algorithms that can not be simplified into each other (a, ab, aba, abab, ababa, ...) so we can't check whether (abab^-1)^2 is a commutator by checking all commutators in G, because there are infinitely many of them

It turns out that there is a set of 12 more simplification rules that you can add into G to get a new set, H, where there are only finitely many (256) algorithms that are different after simplification. i.e. if you take any algorithm, then it can be simplified into exactly 1 of the 256 algorithms in H. e.g. the word s from before can now be simplified into ba^-1ba^2 with these extra simplification rules

If (abab^-1)^2 was a commutator in G, say [x,y], then it would still be a commutator in H, by just interpreting x and y as elements of H and simplifying them with the extra simplification rules. but because H only has finitely many different algorithms, we can just compute [x,y] for all x,y in H, simplify them all, and check if any of them simplify to the same thing that (abab^-1)^2 simplifies to. none of them do. therefore, (abab^-1)^2 is not a commutator in H, and hence also not a commutator in G

content.zh:
换位子

Dylan Wang's video http://youtube.com/watch?v=rZiDvDGHfe8

记号

纯换位子 (pure commutator) [A, B] = A B A' B'; 共轭 (conjugate) A: B = [A: B] = A B A', 称A为预设; 换位子 (转换机, commutator) A: [B, C] = A B C B' C' A'. 称操作A还原某个块, 若A将该块移到该块该去的正确位置 (称为它的家). 换位子能还原所有三循环 (见三循环换位子定理). 本文仅讨论三循环换位子. UF格指的是UF块的顶面格纸; UFR格指的是UFR块的顶面格纸.

纯换

在三循环块中找到用一步操作就能还原的一个块. 若该操作影响且仅影响3个块中的2个块, 则称它为互换 (interchange), 并称第三个块为奇块 (odd piece). 为方便起见, 一般将互换层放在底面. 任意选取奇块上的格作为奇格. 奇格的家上的当前的格称为靶 (target). 称奇块移到靶的操作为插入 (insert) , 若它保留 (即不影响) 互换层上除了靶块的所有块.

纯角

打乱 = x2 y R U' R' D' R U R' D. 互换D'还原DBR块.

方法1是[互换, 插入]. 选取LUF格作为奇格, 它的家为DBR, 故靶为白红绿格. 互换D'将靶移到了DFR, 故插入应该将LUF格移到DFR, R U' R'可作为插入. 解法 = [D', R U' R'].

方法2 = [插入, 互换']. 插入B U2 B'还原LUF块. 解法 = [B U2 B', D].

角换位子可能有中层操作. 例如[F' R' S U', R2].

纯棱

打乱 1 = x2 y F' U L' U2 F B' R2 F' B L U' F. 互换M'还原UF块.

方法1是[互换, 插入]. 选取DR格作为奇格, 它的家为FU, 故靶为黄橙格. 互换M'将靶移到了UB, 故插入应该将DR格移到UB, U R2 U'可作为插入. 解法 = [M', U R2 U'].

方法2 = [插入, 互换']. 插入F R F'还原DR块. 解法 = [F R F', M].

打乱 2 = x2 y B2 R B R' L U' R' U R L' B. 互换U还原UF块.

方法1是[互换, 插入]. 选取BL格作为奇格, 它的家为UR, 故靶为黄红格. 互换U将靶移到了UF, 故插入应该将BL移到UF, U R2 U'可作为插入. 解法 = [M', U R2 U'].

方法2 = [插入, 互换']. 插入R' E2 R还原DBL块. 解法 = [R' E2 R, U'].

棱换位子可能无中层操作. 例如[U R U' R', D' R' D R].

共轭换

若无可行的互换, 或纯换不好, 则考虑共轭换.

打乱1 = x2 y R' F R' B2 R F' R' B2 R2 x. U'不能作为互换因为它影响了所有三个块, 尽管它还原了UFR块. 事实上, 这里无可行的互换. 然而在预设R2后, 互换可以是D2, 因为它还原了LBD块. 使用方法1, 插入可以是R U R'. 解法 = R2: [D2, R U R'].

打乱2 = x2 y U2 L U2 D B U B' D' B U' B' U2 L' U2. 无预设的解 = [D', R U2 R' U' R U R'] (16), 有预设的解1 = U: [R' B' R, F2] (10), 有预设的解2 = U: [F2, U B2 U'] = U F2 U B2 U' F2 U B2 U2 (10-1).

R U F U F B' R2 F' B U F' U' R'
